seoTitle: How to Use the HubSpot API: 2026 Guide
metaDescription: Learn how HubSpot's date-based API works, from generating Private App tokens to fetching CRM records with batch requests and handling rate limits.
intro: HubSpot moved from numbered API versions to date-based endpoints, which changes how you structure requests if you're used to the old system. This guide covers setting up a Private App for authentication, building requests against the new endpoint paths, and handling pagination and rate limits. It's meant for developers building or maintaining integrations against HubSpot's CRM.

steps.heading: Understand the date-based versioning shift
steps.body: Know that HubSpot now uses date-based endpoint paths instead of numeric versions, so older integration code may need updating.

steps.heading: Set up a Private App
steps.body: Go to your account settings, open Integrations, and create a Private App or configure OAuth scopes for your integration.

steps.heading: Generate a bearer token
steps.body: Create your Service Key or use the token endpoint to generate a bearer authentication string for your requests.

steps.heading: Build your request payloads
steps.body: Construct REST calls against the updated endpoint paths to pull or update objects like Contacts and Deals.

steps.heading: Handle pagination and batching
steps.body: Use pagination parameters and batch requests so you don't run into strict API rate limits when working with large datasets.

steps.heading: Plan for rate limit errors
steps.body: Set up exponential backoff retry logic to gracefully handle 429 rate limit responses.

faq.question: What changed with HubSpot's API versioning?
faq.answer: HubSpot moved from numeric versions like v3 to date-based endpoints, so integrations need to reference the correct dated path.

faq.question: How do I authenticate HubSpot API requests?
faq.answer: You generate a bearer token through a Private App or OAuth flow and pass it in the Authorization header of each request.

faq.question: How many records can I fetch in one batch call?
faq.answer: Batch endpoints typically let you fetch up to 100 records per request, which helps you stay within rate limits.

faq.question: What should I do if I get a 429 error?
faq.answer: Implement an exponential backoff retry strategy so your integration waits and retries instead of failing outright.
